Additionally, the assertion that monitoring can promote equitable access overlooks the potential for bias and inequality in resource distribution, undermining the purpose of evidence-based policy. From a healthcare perspective, integrating transaction monitoring capabilities into central bank digital currencies can significantly enhance evidence-based policy sustainability. Transaction monitoring enhances the quality of data related to healthcare funding allocation, which can lead to more targeted and effective health interventions. By monitoring financial transactions through CBDCs, healthcare systems can gain insights into spending patterns that align with community health needs, fostering equity in resource distribution. The integration of transaction monitoring not only ensures financial transparency but also helps identify and mitigate instances of fraud within healthcare funding, increasing the sustainability of financial resources. With real-time transaction tracking, policymakers can respond dynamically to emerging healthcare trends, promoting proactive rather than reactive governance within public health. While there is currently no empirical data on CBDC transaction monitoring, historical examples from public health funding demonstrate how improved financial oversight through monitoring enhances policy outcomes. This connection indicates that integrating transaction monitoring can lead to similar advancements in healthcare sustainability. Opponents may argue that transaction monitoring threatens privacy; however, CBDC frameworks can include privacy protections that allow for oversight while safeguarding individual rights. Concerns about the administrative burden of implementing transaction monitoring can be addressed through innovative technologies that automate and streamline these processes, thereby facilitating efficiency. Some may question the need for transaction monitoring in healthcare; however, the ability to analyze financial transactions in real time can uncover insights that are critical for developing effective public health policies. These healthcare considerations show that evidence-based policy is profoundly affected by integrating transaction monitoring into central bank digital currencies, leading to improved sustainability of financial resources in the healthcare system.
